skill.name: monitoring-analytics
skill.description: Monitor Proxmox infrastructure health and performance. Track node statistics, analyze resource utilization, and identify optimization opportunities across your cluster.
skill.summary: Monitor Proxmox cluster health, workload performance, resource utilization, and capacity trends.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and several prose lines as weak cryptography. Manual review found only documentation for Proxmox monitoring workflows, with no executable code, scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Tool Names Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain Markdown inline code for tool names and workflow steps. They do not contain Ruby backtick execution, shell commands, interpolation, or executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and each flagged backtick encloses a Proxmox MCP tool name. No executable language context or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are ordinary monitoring and capacity-planning text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption function, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found monitoring prose at all flagged lines, not cryptographic code. The confidence is high because the skill contains no code files beyond SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Proxmox clusters can hide performance bottlenecks until workloads slow down. This skill guides health checks, utilization analysis, and capacity planning with MCP monitoring tools.
actual_capabilities: Guides cluster health checks using Proxmox resource and node status tools.
Reviews CPU, memory, disk, and network utilization across infrastructure.
Compares VM and container performance to identify heavy workloads.
Supports bottleneck analysis for nodes, virtual machines, and containers.
Helps create capacity planning reports from current utilization trends.
limitations: Requires configured Proxmox MCP tools and access to the target cluster.
Does not collect metrics by itself without connected monitoring tools.
Historical trend quality depends on available metric history.
Recommendations should be reviewed before changing production workloads.

use_cases.title: Daily Cluster Health Review
use_cases.description: Check cluster resources, node status, and workload health before user impact appears.

use_cases.title: Performance Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Find overloaded nodes, high-consumption VMs, and containers that may need tuning.

use_cases.title: Capacity Planning
use_cases.description: Use current utilization and growth trends to plan host upgrades or workload movement.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check current Proxmox cluster health. Summarize node status, resource utilization, and any workloads that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Workload Utilization Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review VM and container performance across the cluster. Identify the highest CPU, memory, disk, and network consumers.

prompt_templates.title: Bottleneck Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate performance bottlenecks in the Proxmox cluster. Compare node metrics with VM and container usage, then recommend likely fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Capacity Planning Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a capacity planning report from current Proxmox metrics. Include utilization trends, risk areas, and upgrade or rebalancing recommendations.

output_examples.input: What is the current cluster health and performance?
output_examples.output: The cluster is healthy overall. One node has elevated memory pressure, and two VMs are driving most CPU usage.

output_examples.input: Which containers are consuming the most resources?
output_examples.output: The report ranks containers by CPU, memory, disk, and network usage, then highlights candidates for tuning or migration.
The analysis identifies bottleneck type, affected workloads, likely cause, and a prioritized remediation plan.

best_practices: Establish baseline metrics before judging workload behavior.
Review node, VM, and container metrics together before making changes.
Keep historical utilization data for trend analysis and planning.
anti_patterns: Do not move workloads based on a single short metric spike.
Do not ignore storage and network metrics during CPU analysis.
Do not apply tuning recommendations without validating production impact.

faq.question: Does this skill monitor Proxmox directly?
faq.answer: It guides monitoring through available Proxmox MCP tools. It does not collect metrics without those tools.

faq.question: Can it analyze both VMs and containers?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow includes VM status and container status analysis.

faq.question: Can it find performance bottlenecks?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares cluster, node, VM, and container metrics to identify likely bottlenecks.

faq.question: Can it create capacity plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports capacity planning based on utilization, growth trends, and scaling needs.

faq.question: Does it require historical metrics?
faq.answer: Historical metrics improve trend analysis, but current metrics can still support point-in-time reviews.

faq.question: Is it safe for production environments?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only and does not run code. Production changes should still follow normal review controls.
